npcs.name: Aunty Marjorie
lines: This Country has held our songs for many thousands of years.
Listen to the wind through the river red gums. That is language too.
Every hill, every waterhole, every stone has a story waiting to be heard.
npcs.farewell: Walk softly on Country. Carry what you learned with care.

npcs.name: Ranger David
npcs.greeting: G'day. Out walking the tracks today?
lines: We look after this Country together, old ways and new ways side by side.
Watch for the small things. A fresh track, a scarred tree, a bent reed.
When you see kangaroos resting in the shade, you are reading the weather.
npcs.farewell: Stay on the paths, and enjoy your walk.

npcs.name: Jarrah
npcs.greeting: Hey! Quiet around the water, eh?
lines: My grandfather taught me to read the river before I cast a line.
We only take what we need. The rest belongs to tomorrow.
The eel traps at Budj Bim are older than most things you've heard of.
npcs.farewell: If the fish aren't biting, just enjoy the sound of the water.

npcs.name: Nan Rosie
npcs.greeting: Come closer, love. Let me show you what my hands are doing.
lines: I weave with reeds from the wetlands. Each basket remembers the place it came from.
My grandmother taught me. Her grandmother taught her. That's how we stay connected.
When you make something slowly, you put a piece of Country into it.
npcs.farewell: Take your time out there. Country rewards the patient eye.
